summary: Comprehensive testing of ImageKit.io cloud storage integration for Kaizer News app. All upload endpoints working correctly, returning full ImageKit CDN URLs. Frontend FileUpload component properly uses ImageKit URLs directly without prepending backend URL.

passed_tests: POST /api/upload/image - Upload JPEG image returns ImageKit URL (https://ik.imagekit.io/rokudigitals/kaizer-news/images/...)
POST /api/upload/image - Upload PNG image returns ImageKit URL
POST /api/upload/image - Rejects non-image files (text/plain) with 400 status
POST /api/upload/image - Rejects PDF files with 400 status
POST /api/upload/video - Upload MP4 video returns ImageKit URL (https://ik.imagekit.io/rokudigitals/kaizer-news/videos/...)
POST /api/upload/video - Rejects non-video files with 400 status
POST /api/upload/video - Rejects image files when uploading as video
Uploaded ImageKit URL is accessible via HTTP GET (returns 200)
Admin panel Push News form - FileUpload component visible and functional
Admin panel - Image upload to ImageKit works correctly
Admin panel - ImageKit URL preview displays correctly after upload
News creation with ImageKit image URL works correctly
API health check - Root endpoint returns 200
Categories endpoint returns all 10 news categories

critical_code_review_comments: ImageKit SDK v5 initialization is correct (using private_key only)
Upload endpoints properly use imagekit.files.upload() with correct parameters
Response correctly returns result.url, result.file_id, result.name
FileUpload.jsx correctly uses full ImageKit URL without modification
seed_data_creation: Created test articles with ImageKit URLs during testing (cleaned up after tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: ImageKit integration fully tested and working. Upload endpoints at /api/upload/image and /api/upload/video correctly upload to ImageKit CDN and return full URLs starting with https://ik.imagekit.io/rokudigitals/. The local /app/uploads directory is no longer used. FileUpload.jsx uses ImageKit URLs directly. MOCKED: YouTube video feed still uses hardcoded data.
